title: How to Withdraw EPF Online
meta_description: Step-by-step guide to withdraw PF online. Learn EPF withdrawal rules, tax implications, partial withdrawal for home loan, medical, and unemployment.

hero.one_liner: Withdraw your Employee Provident Fund money online through the EPFO portal — complete guide with rules and tax implications

sections.heading: What is EPF?
content: Employee Provident Fund (EPF) is a retirement savings scheme where both you and your employer contribute 12% of your basic salary every month. The money earns 8.25% interest (2025-26 rate) and accumulates over your working years. It's managed by EPFO (Employees' Provident Fund Organisation).

You can withdraw your EPF partially during employment (for specific reasons like home purchase, medical emergency, education) or fully after leaving a job. Full withdrawal is also allowed at retirement (age 58) or if you're unemployed for 2+ months.

rows.label: Unemployment
rows.value: Full withdrawal after 2 months of leaving job (75% after 1 month)

rows.label: Home purchase
rows.value: Partial withdrawal after 5 years of service

rows.label: Home loan repayment
rows.value: Partial withdrawal after 10 years of service

rows.label: Medical emergency
rows.value: Partial withdrawal anytime (for self, spouse, children, parents)

rows.label: Marriage
rows.value: Partial withdrawal after 7 years of service

rows.label: Education
rows.value: Partial withdrawal after 7 years of service (for self or children)

steps.title: Login to EPFO member portal
steps.description: Go to unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in. Login with your UAN (Universal Account Number) and password. If you don't know your UAN, check your salary slip or ask HR.

steps.title: Ensure KYC is complete
steps.description: Go to Manage > KYC. Your Aadhaar, PAN, and bank account must be verified and approved by your employer. This is mandatory for online withdrawal.

steps.title: Submit withdrawal claim
steps.description: Go to Online Services > Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C). Select your verified bank account. Choose the type of claim: Full withdrawal (Form 19) if you've left the job, or Partial withdrawal (Form 31) for specific purposes.

steps.title: Enter details and upload documents
steps.description: For partial withdrawal, select the purpose (home, medical, education, etc.) and enter the required amount. Upload supporting documents if asked.

steps.title: Track your claim
steps.description: Go to Online Services > Track Claim Status. Processing typically takes 10-20 days. Money is credited directly to your linked bank account.

faqs.question: Is EPF withdrawal taxable?
faqs.answer: If you withdraw after 5 years of continuous service, it's completely tax-free. If before 5 years, the employer's contribution and interest are taxable at your slab rate, and TDS of 10% is deducted if the amount exceeds ₹50,000 (no TDS if you submit Form 15G/15H).

faqs.question: Can I withdraw EPF while still employed?
faqs.answer: Yes, partial withdrawal (called 'advance') is allowed for specific reasons: home purchase/construction, home loan EMI, medical emergency, marriage, education. Each has minimum service requirements (5-10 years typically).

faqs.question: How long does EPF withdrawal take?
faqs.answer: Online claims typically take 10-20 days. If your KYC is complete and employer has approved, it can be as fast as 3-7 days. Physical claims take 30-45 days.

faqs.question: What is UAN?
faqs.answer: Universal Account Number is a 12-digit number assigned to every EPF member. It stays the same throughout your career even if you change jobs. Check your salary slip or login to epfindia.gov.in to find it.

faqs.question: Should I withdraw EPF when changing jobs?
faqs.answer: No, it's better to transfer it to your new employer's EPF account. This preserves your continuous service period (important for tax-free withdrawal after 5 years) and keeps your retirement savings growing. Use the online transfer claim on the EPFO portal.

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. EPF rules change periodically. Verify current rules on epfindia.gov.in. Consult a tax advisor for withdrawal tax implications.
